+def validate_library(library: Dict[str, Any], library_path: str = "library") -> None:

+    """Validate library structure and all namespace definitions."""

+    if not isinstance(library, dict):

+        raise ETLError(

+            "SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED",

+            "ETL_ERROR: 'library' must be an object",

+            library_path

+        )

+

+    for ns, ns_value in library.items():

+        # Namespace names can be any string key

+        if not isinstance(ns_value, dict):

+            raise ETLError(

+                "SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED",

+                "ETL_ERROR: namespace must be an object",

+                f"{library_path}.{ns}"

+            )

+

+        # Each namespace should have 'defs'

+        if "defs" not in ns_value:

+            raise ETLError(

+                "SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED",

+                "ETL_ERROR: 'defs' field is required in namespace",

+                f"{library_path}.{ns}.defs"

+            )

+

+        defs = ns_value["defs"]

+        if not isinstance(defs, dict):

+            raise ETLError(

+                "SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED",

+                "ETL_ERROR: 'defs' must be an object",

+                f"{library_path}.{ns}.defs"

+            )

+

+        # Validate each definition in the namespace

+        for def_name, def_value in defs.items():

+            # Validate def_name matches ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$

+            import re

+            if not isinstance(def_name, str) or not re.match(r'^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$', def_name):

+                raise ETLError(

+                    "SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED",

+                    f"ETL_ERROR: definition name must match ^[A-Za-z_][A-Za-z0-9_]*$",

+                    f"{library_path}.{ns}.defs[{def_name}]"

+                )

+

+            if not isinstance(def_value, dict):

+                raise ETLError(

+                    "SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED",

+                    "ETL_ERROR: definition must be an object",

+                    f"{library_path}.{ns}.defs[{def_name}]"

+                )

+

+            if "steps" not in def_value:

+                raise ETLError(

+                    "SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED",

+                    "ETL_ERROR: 'steps' field is required in definition",

+                    f"{library_path}.{ns}.defs[{def_name}].steps"

+                )

+

+            steps = def_value["steps"]

+            if not isinstance(steps, list):

+                raise ETLError(

+                    "SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED",

+                    "ETL_ERROR: 'steps' must be an array",

+                    f"{library_path}.{ns}.defs[{def_name}].steps"

+                )

+

+            # Validate each step in the definition

+            for i, step in enumerate(steps):

+                validate_step(step, i, f"{library_path}.{ns}.defs[{def_name}]")

+

+

+def validate_compose(compose: List[Dict[str, Any]], library: Dict[str, Any], compose_path: str = "compose") -> None:

+    """Validate compose array and all references and inline steps."""

+    if not isinstance(compose, list):

+        raise ETLError(

+            "SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED",

+            "ETL_ERROR: 'compose' must be an array",

+            compose_path

+        )

+

+    for i, item in enumerate(compose):

+        if not isinstance(item, dict):

+            raise ETLError(

+                "SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED",

+                "ETL_ERROR: compose item must be an object",

+                f"{compose_path}[{i}]"

+            )

+

+        # Check for ref or steps (should have exactly one)

+        has_ref = "ref" in item

+        has_steps = "steps" in item

+

+        if not has_ref and not has_steps:

+            raise ETLError(

+                "SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED",

+                "ETL_ERROR: compose item must have 'ref' or 'steps'",

+                f"{compose_path}[{i}]"

+            )

+

+        if has_ref and has_steps:

+            raise ETLError(

+                "SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED",

+                "ETL_ERROR: compose item cannot have both 'ref' and 'steps'",

+                f"{compose_path}[{i}]"

+            )

+

+        if has_ref:

+            # Validate ref format: "ns:name"

+            ref = item["ref"]

+            if not isinstance(ref, str):

+                raise ETLError(

+                    "SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED",

+                    "ETL_ERROR: 'ref' must be a string",

+                    f"{compose_path}[{i}].ref"

+                )

+

+            if is_empty_after_trim(ref):

+                raise ETLError(

+                    "SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED",

+                    "ETL_ERROR: 'ref' cannot be empty",

+                    f"{compose_path}[{i}].ref"

+                )

+

+            # Validate ref format: exactly one colon

+            parts = ref.split(":")

+            if len(parts) != 2:

+                raise ETLError(

+                    "SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED",

+                    "ETL_ERROR: 'ref' must be in format 'ns:name'",

+                    f"{compose_path}[{i}].ref"

+                )

+

+            ns, def_name = parts

+            if is_empty_after_trim(ns) or is_empty_after_trim(def_name):

+                raise ETLError(

+                    "SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED",

+                    "ETL_ERROR: 'ref' must be in format 'ns:name'",

+                    f"{compose_path}[{i}].ref"

+                )

+

+            # Check namespace exists in library

+            if library is None or ns not in library:

+                raise ETLError(

+                    "UNKNOWN_NAMESPACE",

+                    f"ETL_ERROR: namespace '{ns}' not found in library",

+                    f"{compose_path}[{i}].ref"

+                )

+

+            # Check definition exists in namespace

+            if def_name not in library[ns].get("defs", {}):

+                raise ETLError(

+                    "UNKNOWN_LIB_REF",

+                    f"ETL_ERROR: definition '{def_name}' not found in namespace '{ns}'",

+                    f"{compose_path}[{i}].ref"

+                )

+

+            # Validate params if present

+            if "params" in item:

+                params = item["params"]

+                if not isinstance(params, dict):

+                    raise ETLError(

+                        "SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED",

+                        "ETL_ERROR: 'params' must be an object",

+                        f"{compose_path}[{i}].params"

+                    )

+

+        if has_steps:

+            # Validate inline steps

+            steps = item["steps"]

+            if not isinstance(steps, list):

+                raise ETLError(

+                    "SCHEMA_VALIDATION_FAILED",

+                    "ETL_ERROR: 'steps' must be an array",

+                    f"{compose_path}[{i}].steps"

+                )

+

+            for j, step in enumerate(steps):

+                validate_step(step, j, f"{compose_path}[{i}]")

+

+

+def normalize_compose(compose: List[Dict[str, Any]], library: Dict[str, Any]) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:

+    """Normalize compose array into a flat steps array."""

+    normalized_steps = []

+

+    for item in compose:

+        if "ref" in item:

+            # Expand library reference

+            ref = item["ref"]

+            ns, def_name = ref.split(":")

+            params = item.get("params", {})

+

+            # Get definition steps

+            def_steps = library[ns]["defs"][def_name].get("steps", [])

+

+            for step in def_steps:

+                # Normalize the step and substitute params

+                normalized_step = normalize_step(step)

+

+                # If this is a filter or map with params reference, substitute

+                # For normalization mode (--execute=false), params references remain as expressions

+                # We just add the step as-is

+                normalized_steps.append(normalized_step)

+

+        elif "steps" in item:

+            # Inline steps

+            for step in item["steps"]:

+                normalized_steps.append(normalize_step(step))

+

+    return normalized_steps

+

+

+def execute_compose(data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Tuple[Dict[str, Any], int]:

+    """Execute a compose-based pipeline and return results with metrics."""

+    try:

+        # Validate structure first

+        validate_json_structure(data)

+

+        compose = data["compose"]

+        rows = data["dataset"]

+        rows_in = len(rows)

+

+        # Get library if present and validate it

+        library = data.get("library", {})

+        if library:

+            validate_library(library)

+

+        # Validate compose

+        validate_compose(compose, library)

+

+        # Execute each item in compose

+        for item_idx, item in enumerate(compose):

+            if "ref" in item:

+                # Execute library reference

+                ref = item["ref"]

+                ns, def_name = ref.split(":")

+                params = item.get("params", {})

+

+                # Get definition steps

+                def_steps = library[ns]["defs"][def_name].get("steps", [])

+

+                # Execute each step with params

+                for j, def_step in enumerate(def_steps):

+                    op = def_step.get("op", "").strip().lower()

+                    normalized = normalize_step(def_step)

+

+                    if op == "select":

+                        rows = execute_select(rows, normalized, j, f"library.{ns}.defs[{def_name}].steps")

+                    elif op == "filter":

+                        rows = execute_filter_with_params(rows, normalized, j, f"library.{ns}.defs[{def_name}].steps", params)

+                    elif op == "map":

+                        rows = execute_map_with_params(rows, normalized, j, f"library.{ns}.defs[{def_name}].steps", params)

+                    elif op == "rename":

+                        rows = execute_rename(rows, normalized, j, f"library.{ns}.defs[{def_name}].steps")

+                    elif op == "limit":

+                        rows = execute_limit(rows, normalized, j, f"library.{ns}.defs[{def_name}].steps")

+                    elif op == "branch":

+                        rows = execute_branch_with_params(rows, normalized, j, f"library.{ns}.defs[{def_name}].steps", {}, set(), params)

+

+            elif "steps" in item:

+                # Execute inline steps

+                inline_steps = item["steps"]

+                for j, inline_step in enumerate(inline_steps):

+                    op = inline_step.get("op", "").strip().lower()

+                    normalized = normalize_step(inline_step)

+

+                    if op == "select":

+                        rows = execute_select(rows, normalized, j, f"compose[{item_idx}].steps")

+                    elif op == "filter":

+                        rows = execute_filter_with_params(rows, normalized, j, f"compose[{item_idx}].steps", {})

+                    elif op == "map":

+                        rows = execute_map_with_params(rows, normalized, j, f"compose[{item_idx}].steps", {})

+                    elif op == "rename":

+                        rows = execute_rename(rows, normalized, j, f"compose[{item_idx}].steps")

+                    elif op == "limit":

+                        rows = execute_limit(rows, normalized, j, f"compose[{item_idx}].steps")

+                    elif op == "branch":

+                        rows = execute_branch_with_params(rows, normalized, j, f"compose[{item_idx}].steps", {}, set(), {})

+

+        rows_out = len(rows)

+

+        return {

+            "status": "ok",

+            "data": rows,

+            "metrics": {

+                "rows_in": rows_in,

+                "rows_out": rows_out

+            }

+        }, 0

+

+    except ETLError as e:

+        return {

+            "status": "error",

+            "error_code": e.error_code,

+            "message": e.message,

+            "path": e.path

+        }, 1

+

+
